claim: The unit of analysis for world-war-i-nationalisms is contested: individual belligerent nations varied significantly in their sacrificial mobilization patterns, sacred-political-order strength, and outcomes (German collapse, British restrained-reordering, French hybrid). Treating the case as a unified sacrificial-escalation instance may suppress theoretically important cross-national variation.
rationale: A case covering multiple nations with different outcomes simultaneously tests different theory pathways; disaggregating into individual national cases would allow finer-grained theory-testing but reduces the comparative breadth that motivates including the case.
